verses.esv: Now King David was old and advanced in years. And although they covered him with clothes, he could not get warm.
verses.kjv: NOW king David was old and stricken in years; and they covered him with clothes, but he gat no heat.
verses.bsb: Now King David was old and well along in years, and though they covered him with blankets, he could not keep warm.

verses.esv: Therefore his servants said to him, “Let a young woman be sought for my lord the king, and let her wait on the king and be in his service. Let her lie in your arms, that my lord the king may be warm.”
verses.kjv: Wherefore his servants said unto him, Let there be sought for my lord the king a young virgin: and let her stand before the king, and let her cherish him, and let her lie in thy bosom, that my lord the king may get heat.
verses.bsb: So his servants said to him, “Let us search for a young virgin for our lord the king, to attend to him and care for him and lie by his side to keep him warm.”

verses.esv: So they sought for a beautiful young woman throughout all the territory of Israel, and found Abishag the Shunammite, and brought her to the king.
verses.kjv: So they sought for a fair damsel throughout all the coasts of Israel, and found Abishag a Shunammite, and brought her to the king.
verses.bsb: Then they searched throughout Israel for a beautiful girl, and they found Abishag the Shunammite and brought her to the king.

verses.esv: The young woman was very beautiful, and she was of service to the king and attended to him, but the king knew her not.
verses.kjv: And the damsel was very fair, and cherished the king, and ministered to him: but the king knew her not.
verses.bsb: The girl was unsurpassed in beauty; she cared for the king and served him, but he had no relations with her.

verses.esv: Now Adonijah the son of Haggith exalted himself, saying, “I will be king.” And he prepared for himself chariots and horsemen, and fifty men to run before him.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Adonijah the son of Haggith exalted himself, saying, I will be king: and he prepared him chariots and horsemen, and fifty men to run before him.
verses.bsb: At that time Adonijah, David’s son by Haggith, began to exalt himself, saying, “I will be king!” And he acquired chariots and horsemen and fifty men to run ahead of him.

verses.esv: His father had never at any time displeased him by asking, “Why have you done thus and so?” He was also a very handsome man, and he was born next after Absalom.
verses.kjv: And his father had not displeased him at any time in saying, Why hast thou done so? and he also was a very goodly man; and his mother bare him after Absalom.
verses.bsb: (His father had never once reprimanded him by saying, “Why do you act this way?” Adonijah was also very handsome, born next after Absalom.)

verses.esv: He conferred with Joab the son of Zeruiah and with Abiathar the priest. And they followed Adonijah and helped him.
verses.kjv: And he conferred with Joab the son of Zeruiah, and with Abiathar the priest: and they following Adonijah helped him.
verses.bsb: So Adonijah conferred with Joab son of Zeruiah and with Abiathar the priest, who supported him.

verses.esv: But Zadok the priest and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada and Nathan the prophet and Shimei and Rei and David's mighty men were not with Adonijah.
verses.kjv: But Zadok the priest, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and Nathan the prophet, and Shimei, and Rei, and the mighty men which belonged to David, were not with Adonijah.
verses.bsb: But Zadok the priest, Benaiah son of Jehoiada, Nathan the prophet, Shimei, Rei, and David’s mighty men would not join Adonijah.

verses.esv: Adonijah sacrificed sheep, oxen, and fattened cattle by the Serpent's Stone, which is beside En-rogel, and he invited all his brothers, the king's sons, and all the royal officials of Judah,
verses.kjv: And Adonijah slew sheep and oxen and fat cattle by the stone of Zoheleth, which is by En-rogel, and called all his brethren the king’s sons, and all the men of Judah the king’s servants:
verses.bsb: And Adonijah sacrificed sheep, oxen, and fattened calves near the stone of Zoheleth, which is next to En-rogel. He invited all his royal brothers and all the men of Judah who were servants of the king.

verses.esv: but he did not invite Nathan the prophet or Benaiah or the mighty men or Solomon his brother.
verses.kjv: But Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah, and the mighty men, and Solomon his brother, he called not.
verses.bsb: But he did not invite Nathan the prophet, Benaiah, the mighty men, or his brother Solomon.

verses.esv: Then Nathan said to Bathsheba the mother of Solomon, “Have you not heard that Adonijah the son of Haggith has become king and David our lord does not know it?
verses.kjv: ¶ Wherefore Nathan spake unto Bath-sheba the mother of Solomon, saying, Hast thou not heard that Adonijah the son of Haggith doth reign, and David our lord knoweth it not?
verses.bsb: Then Nathan said to Bathsheba the mother of Solomon, “Have you not heard that Adonijah son of Haggith has become king, and our lord David does not know it?

verses.esv: Now therefore come, let me give you advice, that you may save your own life and the life of your son Solomon.
verses.kjv: Now therefore come, let me, I pray thee, give thee counsel, that thou mayest save thine own life, and the life of thy son Solomon.
verses.bsb: Now please, come and let me advise you. Save your own life and the life of your son Solomon.

verses.esv: Go in at once to King David, and say to him, ‘Did you not, my lord the king, swear to your servant, saying, “Solomon your son shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne”? Why then is Adonijah king?’
verses.kjv: Go and get thee in unto king David, and say unto him, Didst not thou, my lord, O king, swear unto thine handmaid, saying, Assuredly Solomon thy son shall reign after me, and he shall sit upon my throne? why then doth Adonijah reign?
verses.bsb: Go at once to King David and say, ‘My lord the king, did you not swear to your maidservant, “Surely your son Solomon will reign after me, and he will sit on my throne”? Why then has Adonijah become king?’

verses.esv: Then while you are still speaking with the king, I also will come in after you and confirm your words.”
verses.kjv: Behold, while thou yet talkest there with the king, I also will come in after thee, and confirm thy words.
verses.bsb: Then, while you are still there speaking with the king, I will come in after you and confirm your words.”

verses.esv: So Bathsheba went to the king in his chamber (now the king was very old, and Abishag the Shunammite was attending to the king).
verses.kjv: ¶ And Bath-sheba went in unto the king into the chamber: and the king was very old; and Abishag the Shunammite ministered unto the king.
verses.bsb: So Bathsheba went to see the king in his bedroom. Since the king was very old, Abishag the Shunammite was serving him.

verses.esv: Bathsheba bowed and paid homage to the king, and the king said, “What do you desire?”
verses.kjv: And Bath-sheba bowed, and did obeisance unto the king. And the king said, What wouldest thou?
verses.bsb: And Bathsheba bowed down in homage to the king, who asked, “What is your desire?”

verses.esv: She said to him, “My lord, you swore to your servant by the LORD your God, saying, ‘Solomon your son shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne.’
verses.kjv: And she said unto him, My lord, thou swarest by the Lord thy God unto thine handmaid, saying, Assuredly Solomon thy son shall reign after me, and he shall sit upon my throne.
verses.bsb: “My lord,” she replied, “you yourself swore to your maidservant by the LORD your God: ‘Surely your son Solomon will reign after me, and he will sit on my throne.’

verses.esv: And now, behold, Adonijah is king, although you, my lord the king, do not know it.
verses.kjv: And now, behold, Adonijah reigneth; and now, my lord the king, thou knowest it not:
verses.bsb: But now, behold, Adonijah has become king, and you, my lord the king, did not know it.

verses.esv: He has sacrificed oxen, fattened cattle, and sheep in abundance, and has invited all the sons of the king, Abiathar the priest, and Joab the commander of the army, but Solomon your servant he has not invited.
verses.kjv: And he hath slain oxen and fat cattle and sheep in abundance, and hath called all the sons of the king, and Abiathar the priest, and Joab the captain of the host: but Solomon thy servant hath he not called.
verses.bsb: And he has sacrificed an abundance of oxen, fattened calves, and sheep, and has invited all the other sons of the king, as well as Abiathar the priest and Joab the commander of the army. But he did not invite your servant Solomon.

verses.esv: And now, my lord the king, the eyes of all Israel are on you, to tell them who shall sit on the throne of my lord the king after him.
verses.kjv: And thou, my lord, O king, the eyes of all Israel are upon thee, that thou shouldest tell them who shall sit on the throne of my lord the king after him.
verses.bsb: And as for you, my lord the king, the eyes of all Israel are upon you to tell them who will sit on the throne of my lord the king after him.

verses.esv: Otherwise it will come to pass, when my lord the king sleeps with his fathers, that I and my son Solomon will be counted offenders.”
verses.kjv: Otherwise it shall come to pass, when my lord the king shall sleep with his fathers, that I and my son Solomon shall be counted offenders.
verses.bsb: Otherwise, when my lord the king rests with his fathers, I and my son Solomon will be counted as criminals.”

verses.esv: While she was still speaking with the king, Nathan the prophet came in.
verses.kjv: ¶ And, lo, while she yet talked with the king, Nathan the prophet also came in.
verses.bsb: And just then, while Bathsheba was still speaking with the king, Nathan the prophet arrived.

verses.esv: And they told the king, “Here is Nathan the prophet.” And when he came in before the king, he bowed before the king, with his face to the ground.
verses.kjv: And they told the king, saying, Behold Nathan the prophet. And when he was come in before the king, he bowed himself before the king with his face to the ground.
verses.bsb: So the king was told, “Nathan the prophet is here.” And Nathan went in and bowed facedown before the king.

verses.esv: And Nathan said, “My lord the king, have you said, ‘Adonijah shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne’?
verses.kjv: And Nathan said, My lord, O king, hast thou said, Adonijah shall reign after me, and he shall sit upon my throne?
verses.bsb: “My lord the king,” said Nathan, “did you say, ‘Adonijah will reign after me, and he will sit on my throne’?

verses.esv: For he has gone down this day and has sacrificed oxen, fattened cattle, and sheep in abundance, and has invited all the king's sons, the commanders of the army, and Abiathar the priest. And behold, they are eating and drinking before him, and saying, ‘Long live King Adonijah!’
verses.kjv: For he is gone down this day, and hath slain oxen and fat cattle and sheep in abundance, and hath called all the king’s sons, and the captains of the host, and Abiathar the priest; and, behold, they eat and drink before him, and say, God save king Adonijah.
verses.bsb: For today he has gone down and sacrificed an abundance of oxen, fattened calves, and sheep, and has invited all the sons of the king, the commanders of the army, and Abiathar the priest. And behold, they are eating and drinking before him, saying, ‘Long live King Adonijah!’

verses.esv: But me, your servant, and Zadok the priest, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and your servant Solomon he has not invited.
verses.kjv: But me, even me thy servant, and Zadok the priest, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and thy servant Solomon, hath he not called.
verses.bsb: But me your servant he did not invite, nor Zadok the priest, nor Benaiah son of Jehoiada, nor your servant Solomon.

verses.esv: Has this thing been brought about by my lord the king and you have not told your servants who should sit on the throne of my lord the king after him?”
verses.kjv: Is this thing done by my lord the king, and thou hast not shewed it unto thy servant, who should sit on the throne of my lord the king after him?
verses.bsb: Has my lord the king let this happen without informing your servant who should sit on the throne after my lord the king?”

verses.esv: Then King David answered, “Call Bathsheba to me.” So she came into the king's presence and stood before the king.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then king David answered and said, Call me Bath-sheba. And she came into the king’s presence, and stood before the king.
verses.bsb: Then King David said, “Call in Bathsheba for me.” So she came into the king’s presence and stood before him.

verses.esv: And the king swore, saying, “As the LORD lives, who has redeemed my soul out of every adversity,
verses.kjv: And the king sware, and said, As the Lord liveth, that hath redeemed my soul out of all distress,
verses.bsb: And the king swore an oath, saying, “As surely as the LORD lives, who has redeemed my life from all distress,

verses.esv: as I swore to you by the LORD, the God of Israel, saying, ‘Solomon your son shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne in my place,’ even so will I do this day.”
verses.kjv: Even as I sware unto thee by the Lord God of Israel, saying, Assuredly Solomon thy son shall reign after me, and he shall sit upon my throne in my stead; even so will I certainly do this day.
verses.bsb: I will carry out this very day exactly what I swore to you by the LORD, the God of Israel: Surely your son Solomon will reign after me, and he will sit on my throne in my place.”

verses.esv: Then Bathsheba bowed with her face to the ground and paid homage to the king and said, “May my lord King David live forever!”
verses.kjv: Then Bath-sheba bowed with her face to the earth, and did reverence to the king, and said, Let my lord king David live for ever.
verses.bsb: Bathsheba bowed facedown in homage to the king and said, “May my lord King David live forever!”

verses.esv: King David said, “Call to me Zadok the priest, Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada.” So they came before the king.
verses.kjv: ¶ And king David said, Call me Zadok the priest, and Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada. And they came before the king.
verses.bsb: Then King David said, “Call in for me Zadok the priest, Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada.” So they came before the king.

verses.esv: And the king said to them, “Take with you the servants of your lord and have Solomon my son ride on my own mule, and bring him down to Gihon.
verses.kjv: The king also said unto them, Take with you the servants of your lord, and cause Solomon my son to ride upon mine own mule, and bring him down to Gihon:
verses.bsb: “Take my servants with you,” said the king. “Set my son Solomon on my own mule and take him down to Gihon.

verses.esv: And let Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet there anoint him king over Israel. Then blow the trumpet and say, ‘Long live King Solomon!’
verses.kjv: And let Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet anoint him there king over Israel: and blow ye with the trumpet, and say, God save king Solomon.
verses.bsb: There Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet are to anoint him king over Israel. You are to blow the ram’s horn and declare, ‘Long live King Solomon!’

verses.esv: You shall then come up after him, and he shall come and sit on my throne, for he shall be king in my place. And I have appointed him to be ruler over Israel and over Judah.”
verses.kjv: Then ye shall come up after him, that he may come and sit upon my throne; for he shall be king in my stead: and I have appointed him to be ruler over Israel and over Judah.
verses.bsb: Then you shall go up with him, and he is to come and sit on my throne and reign in my place. For I have appointed him ruler over Israel and Judah.”

verses.esv: And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada answered the king, “Amen! May the LORD, the God of my lord the king, say so.
verses.kjv: And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada answered the king, and said, Amen: the Lord God of my lord the king say so too.
verses.bsb: “Amen,” replied Benaiah son of Jehoiada. “May the LORD, the God of my lord the king, so declare it.

verses.esv: As the LORD has been with my lord the king, even so may he be with Solomon, and make his throne greater than the throne of my lord King David.”
verses.kjv: As the Lord hath been with my lord the king, even so be he with Solomon, and make his throne greater than the throne of my lord king David.
verses.bsb: Just as the LORD was with my lord the king, so may He be with Solomon and make his throne even greater than that of my lord King David.”

verses.esv: So Zadok the priest, Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and the Cherethites and the Pelethites went down and had Solomon ride on King David's mule and brought him to Gihon.
verses.kjv: So Zadok the priest, and Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and the Cherethites, and the Pelethites, went down, and caused Solomon to ride upon king David’s mule, and brought him to Gihon.
verses.bsb: Then Zadok the priest, Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada, along with the Cherethites and Pelethites, went down and set Solomon on King David’s mule, and they escorted him to Gihon.

verses.esv: There Zadok the priest took the horn of oil from the tent and anointed Solomon. Then they blew the trumpet, and all the people said, “Long live King Solomon!”
verses.kjv: And Zadok the priest took an horn of oil out of the tabernacle, and anointed Solomon. And they blew the trumpet; and all the people said, God save king Solomon.
verses.bsb: Zadok the priest took the horn of oil from the tabernacle and anointed Solomon. Then they blew the ram’s horn, and all the people proclaimed, “Long live King Solomon!”

verses.esv: And all the people went up after him, playing on pipes, and rejoicing with great joy, so that the earth was split by their noise.
verses.kjv: And all the people came up after him, and the people piped with pipes, and rejoiced with great joy, so that the earth rent with the sound of them.
verses.bsb: All the people followed him, playing flutes and rejoicing with such a great joy that the earth was split by the sound.

verses.esv: Adonijah and all the guests who were with him heard it as they finished feasting. And when Joab heard the sound of the trumpet, he said, “What does this uproar in the city mean?”
verses.kjv: ¶ And Adonijah and all the guests that were with him heard it as they had made an end of eating. And when Joab heard the sound of the trumpet, he said, Wherefore is this noise of the city being in an uproar?
verses.bsb: Now Adonijah and all his guests were finishing their feast when they heard the sound of the ram’s horn. “Why is the city in such a loud uproar?” asked Joab.

verses.esv: While he was still speaking, behold, Jonathan the son of Abiathar the priest came. And Adonijah said, “Come in, for you are a worthy man and bring good news.”
verses.kjv: And while he yet spake, behold, Jonathan the son of Abiathar the priest came: and Adonijah said unto him, Come in; for thou art a valiant man, and bringest good tidings.
verses.bsb: As he was speaking, suddenly Jonathan the son of Abiathar the priest arrived. “Come in,” said Adonijah, “for you are a man of valor. You must be bringing good news.”

verses.esv: Jonathan answered Adonijah, “No, for our lord King David has made Solomon king,
verses.kjv: And Jonathan answered and said to Adonijah, Verily our lord king David hath made Solomon king.
verses.bsb: “Not at all,” Jonathan replied. “Our lord King David has made Solomon king.

verses.esv: and the king has sent with him Zadok the priest, Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and the Cherethites and the Pelethites. And they had him ride on the king's mule.
verses.kjv: And the king hath sent with him Zadok the priest, and Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and the Cherethites, and the Pelethites, and they have caused him to ride upon the king’s mule:
verses.bsb: And with Solomon, the king has sent Zadok the priest, Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada, along with the Cherethites and Pelethites, and they have set him on the king’s mule.

verses.esv: And Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet have anointed him king at Gihon, and they have gone up from there rejoicing, so that the city is in an uproar. This is the noise that you have heard.
verses.kjv: And Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet have anointed him king in Gihon: and they are come up from thence rejoicing, so that the city rang again. This is the noise that ye have heard.
verses.bsb: Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet have anointed him king at Gihon, and they have gone up from there with rejoicing that rings out in the city. That is the noise you hear.

verses.esv: Solomon sits on the royal throne.
verses.kjv: And also Solomon sitteth on the throne of the kingdom.
verses.bsb: Moreover, Solomon has taken his seat on the royal throne.

verses.esv: Moreover, the king's servants came to congratulate our lord King David, saying, ‘May your God make the name of Solomon more famous than yours, and make his throne greater than your throne.’ And the king bowed himself on the bed.
verses.kjv: And moreover the king’s servants came to bless our lord king David, saying, God make the name of Solomon better than thy name, and make his throne greater than thy throne. And the king bowed himself upon the bed.
verses.bsb: The king’s servants have also gone to congratulate our lord King David, saying, ‘May your God make the name of Solomon more famous than your own name, and may He make his throne greater than your throne.’  And the king has bowed in worship on his bed,

verses.esv: And the king also said, ‘Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, who has granted someone to sit on my throne this day, my own eyes seeing it.’”
verses.kjv: And also thus said the king, Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, which hath given one to sit on my throne this day, mine eyes even seeing it.
verses.bsb: saying, ‘Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel! Today He has provided one to sit on my throne, and my eyes have seen it.’”

verses.esv: Then all the guests of Adonijah trembled and rose, and each went his own way.
verses.kjv: And all the guests that were with Adonijah were afraid, and rose up, and went every man his way.
verses.bsb: At this, all the guests of Adonijah arose in terror and scattered.

verses.esv: And Adonijah feared Solomon. So he arose and went and took hold of the horns of the altar.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Adonijah feared because of Solomon, and arose, and went, and caught hold on the horns of the altar.
verses.bsb: But Adonijah, in fear of Solomon, got up and went to take hold of the horns of the altar.

verses.esv: Then it was told Solomon, “Behold, Adonijah fears King Solomon, for behold, he has laid hold of the horns of the altar, saying, ‘Let King Solomon swear to me first that he will not put his servant to death with the sword.’”
verses.kjv: And it was told Solomon, saying, Behold, Adonijah feareth king Solomon: for, lo, he hath caught hold on the horns of the altar, saying, Let king Solomon swear unto me to day that he will not slay his servant with the sword.
verses.bsb: It was reported to Solomon: “Behold, Adonijah fears King Solomon, and he has taken hold of the horns of the altar, saying, ‘Let King Solomon first swear to me not to put his servant to the sword.’”

verses.esv: And Solomon said, “If he will show himself a worthy man, not one of his hairs shall fall to the earth, but if wickedness is found in him, he shall die.”
verses.kjv: And Solomon said, If he will shew himself a worthy man, there shall not an hair of him fall to the earth: but if wickedness shall be found in him, he shall die.
verses.bsb: And Solomon replied, “If he is a man of character, not a single hair of his will fall to the ground. But if evil is found in him, he will die.”

verses.esv: So King Solomon sent, and they brought him down from the altar. And he came and paid homage to King Solomon, and Solomon said to him, “Go to your house.”
verses.kjv: So king Solomon sent, and they brought him down from the altar. And he came and bowed himself to king Solomon: and Solomon said unto him, Go to thine house.
verses.bsb: So King Solomon summoned Adonijah down from the altar, and he came and bowed down before King Solomon, who said to him, “Go to your home.”

verses.esv: When David's time to die drew near, he commanded Solomon his son, saying,
verses.kjv: NOW the days of David drew nigh that he should die; and he charged Solomon his son, saying,
verses.bsb: As the time drew near for David to die, he charged his son Solomon,

verses.esv: “I am about to go the way of all the earth. Be strong, and show yourself a man,
verses.kjv: I go the way of all the earth: be thou strong therefore, and shew thyself a man;
verses.bsb: “I am about to go the way of all the earth. So be strong and prove yourself a man.

verses.esv: and keep the charge of the LORD your God, walking in his ways and keeping his statutes, his commandments, his rules, and his testimonies, as it is written in the Law of Moses, that you may prosper in all that you do and wherever you turn,
verses.kjv: And keep the charge of the Lord thy God, to walk in his ways, to keep his statutes, and his commandments, and his judgments, and his testimonies, as it is written in the law of Moses, that thou mayest prosper in all that thou doest, and whithersoever thou turnest thyself:
verses.bsb: And keep the charge of the LORD your God to walk in His ways and to keep His statutes, commandments, ordinances, and decrees, as is written in the Law of Moses, so that you may prosper in all you do and wherever you turn,

verses.esv: that the LORD may establish his word that he spoke concerning me, saying, ‘If your sons pay close attention to their way, to walk before me in faithfulness with all their heart and with all their soul, you shall not lack a man on the throne of Israel.’
verses.kjv: That the Lord may continue his word which he spake concerning me, saying, If thy children take heed to their way, to walk before me in truth with all their heart and with all their soul, there shall not fail thee (said he) a man on the throne of Israel.
verses.bsb: and so that the LORD may fulfill His promise to me: ‘If your descendants take heed to walk faithfully before Me with all their heart and soul, you will never fail to have a man on the throne of Israel.’

verses.esv: “Moreover, you also know what Joab the son of Zeruiah did to me, how he dealt with the two commanders of the armies of Israel, Abner the son of Ner, and Amasa the son of Jether, whom he killed, avenging in time of peace for blood that had been shed in war, and putting the blood of war on the belt around his waist and on the sandals on his feet.
verses.kjv: Moreover thou knowest also what Joab the son of Zeruiah did to me, and what he did to the two captains of the hosts of Israel, unto Abner the son of Ner, and unto Amasa the son of Jether, whom he slew, and shed the blood of war in peace, and put the blood of war upon his girdle that was about his loins, and in his shoes that were on his feet.
verses.bsb: Moreover, you know what Joab son of Zeruiah did to me—what he did to Abner son of Ner and Amasa son of Jether, the two commanders of the armies of Israel. He killed them in peacetime to avenge the blood of war. He stained with the blood of war the belt around his waist and the sandals on his feet.

verses.esv: Act therefore according to your wisdom, but do not let his gray head go down to Sheol in peace.
verses.kjv: Do therefore according to thy wisdom, and let not his hoar head go down to the grave in peace.
verses.bsb: So act according to your wisdom, and do not let his gray head go down to Sheol in peace.

verses.esv: But deal loyally with the sons of Barzillai the Gileadite, and let them be among those who eat at your table, for with such loyalty they met me when I fled from Absalom your brother.
verses.kjv: But shew kindness unto the sons of Barzillai the Gileadite, and let them be of those that eat at thy table: for so they came to me when I fled because of Absalom thy brother.
verses.bsb: But show loving devotion to the sons of Barzillai the Gileadite, and let them be among those who eat at your table, because they stood by me when I fled from your brother Absalom.

verses.esv: And there is also with you Shimei the son of Gera, the Benjaminite from Bahurim, who cursed me with a grievous curse on the day when I went to Mahanaim. But when he came down to meet me at the Jordan, I swore to him by the LORD, saying, ‘I will not put you to death with the sword.’
verses.kjv: And, behold, thou hast with thee Shimei the son of Gera, a Benjamite of Bahurim, which cursed me with a grievous curse in the day when I went to Mahanaim: but he came down to meet me at Jordan, and I sware to him by the Lord, saying, I will not put thee to death with the sword.
verses.bsb: Keep an eye on Shimei the son of Gera, the Benjamite from Bahurim who is with you. He called down bitter curses against me on the day I went to Mahanaim, but when he came down to meet me at the Jordan, I swore to him by the LORD: ‘I will never put you to the sword.’

verses.esv: Now therefore do not hold him guiltless, for you are a wise man. You will know what you ought to do to him, and you shall bring his gray head down with blood to Sheol.”
verses.kjv: Now therefore hold him not guiltless: for thou art a wise man, and knowest what thou oughtest to do unto him; but his hoar head bring thou down to the grave with blood.
verses.bsb: Now therefore, do not hold him guiltless, for you are a wise man. You know what you ought to do to him to bring his gray head down to Sheol in blood.”

verses.esv: Then David slept with his fathers and was buried in the city of David.
verses.kjv: So David slept with his fathers, and was buried in the city of David.
verses.bsb: Then David rested with his fathers and was buried in the City of David.

verses.esv: And the time that David reigned over Israel was forty years. He reigned seven years in Hebron and thirty-three years in Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: And the days that David reigned over Israel were forty years: seven years reigned he in Hebron, and thirty and three years reigned he in Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: The length of David’s reign over Israel was forty years—seven years in Hebron and thirty-three years in Jerusalem.

verses.esv: So Solomon sat on the throne of David his father, and his kingdom was firmly established.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then sat Solomon upon the throne of David his father; and his kingdom was established greatly.
verses.bsb: So Solomon sat on the throne of his father David, and his kingdom was firmly established.

verses.esv: Then Adonijah the son of Haggith came to Bathsheba the mother of Solomon. And she said, “Do you come peacefully?” He said, “Peacefully.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And Adonijah the son of Haggith came to Bath-sheba the mother of Solomon. And she said, Comest thou peaceably? And he said, Peaceably.
verses.bsb: Now Adonijah son of Haggith went to Bathsheba the mother of Solomon, and she asked, “Do you come in peace?”  “Yes, in peace,” he replied.

verses.esv: Then he said, “I have something to say to you.” She said, “Speak.”
verses.kjv: He said moreover, I have somewhat to say unto thee. And she said, Say on.
verses.bsb: Then he said, “I have something to tell you.”  “Say it,” she answered.

verses.esv: He said, “You know that the kingdom was mine, and that all Israel fully expected me to reign. However, the kingdom has turned about and become my brother's, for it was his from the LORD.
verses.kjv: And he said, Thou knowest that the kingdom was mine, and that all Israel set their faces on me, that I should reign: howbeit the kingdom is turned about, and is become my brother’s: for it was his from the Lord.
verses.bsb: “You know that the kingship was mine,” he said. “All Israel expected that I should reign, but the kingship has turned to my brother, for it has come to him from the LORD.

verses.esv: And now I have one request to make of you; do not refuse me.” She said to him, “Speak.”
verses.kjv: And now I ask one petition of thee, deny me not. And she said unto him, Say on.
verses.bsb: So now I have just one request of you; do not deny me.”  “State your request,” she told him.

verses.esv: And he said, “Please ask King Solomon—he will not refuse you—to give me Abishag the Shunammite as my wife.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Speak, I pray thee, unto Solomon the king, (for he will not say thee nay,) that he give me Abishag the Shunammite to wife.
verses.bsb: Adonijah replied, “Please speak to King Solomon, since he will not turn you down. Let him give me Abishag the Shunammite as my wife.”

verses.esv: Bathsheba said, “Very well; I will speak for you to the king.”
verses.kjv: And Bath-sheba said, Well; I will speak for thee unto the king.
verses.bsb: “Very well,” Bathsheba replied. “I will speak to the king for you.”

verses.esv: So Bathsheba went to King Solomon to speak to him on behalf of Adonijah. And the king rose to meet her and bowed down to her. Then he sat on his throne and had a seat brought for the king's mother, and she sat on his right.
verses.kjv: ¶ Bath-sheba therefore went unto king Solomon, to speak unto him for Adonijah. And the king rose up to meet her, and bowed himself unto her, and sat down on his throne, and caused a seat to be set for the king’s mother; and she sat on his right hand.
verses.bsb: So Bathsheba went to King Solomon to speak to him for Adonijah. The king stood up to greet her, bowed to her, and sat down on his throne. Then the king had a throne brought for his mother, who sat down at his right hand.

verses.esv: Then she said, “I have one small request to make of you; do not refuse me.” And the king said to her, “Make your request, my mother, for I will not refuse you.”
verses.kjv: Then she said, I desire one small petition of thee; I pray thee, say me not nay. And the king said unto her, Ask on, my mother: for I will not say thee nay.
verses.bsb: “I have just one small request of you,” she said. “Do not deny me.”  “Make your request, my mother,” the king replied, “for I will not deny you.”

verses.esv: She said, “Let Abishag the Shunammite be given to Adonijah your brother as his wife.”
verses.kjv: And she said, Let Abishag the Shunammite be given to Adonijah thy brother to wife.
verses.bsb: So Bathsheba said, “Let Abishag the Shunammite be given to your brother Adonijah as his wife.”

verses.esv: King Solomon answered his mother, “And why do you ask Abishag the Shunammite for Adonijah? Ask for him the kingdom also, for he is my older brother, and on his side are Abiathar the priest and Joab the son of Zeruiah.”
verses.kjv: And king Solomon answered and said unto his mother, And why dost thou ask Abishag the Shunammite for Adonijah? ask for him the kingdom also; for he is mine elder brother; even for him, and for Abiathar the priest, and for Joab the son of Zeruiah.
verses.bsb: King Solomon answered his mother, “Why do you request Abishag the Shunammite for Adonijah? Since he is my older brother, you might as well request the kingdom for him and for Abiathar the priest and for Joab son of Zeruiah!”

verses.esv: Then King Solomon swore by the LORD, saying, “God do so to me and more also if this word does not cost Adonijah his life!
verses.kjv: Then king Solomon sware by the Lord, saying, God do so to me, and more also, if Adonijah have not spoken this word against his own life.
verses.bsb: Then King Solomon swore by the LORD: “May God punish me, and ever so severely, if Adonijah has not made this request at the expense of his life.

verses.esv: Now therefore as the LORD lives, who has established me and placed me on the throne of David my father, and who has made me a house, as he promised, Adonijah shall be put to death today.”
verses.kjv: Now therefore, as the Lord liveth, which hath established me, and set me on the throne of David my father, and who hath made me an house, as he promised, Adonijah shall be put to death this day.
verses.bsb: And now, as surely as the LORD lives—the One who established me, who set me on the throne of my father David, and who founded for me a dynasty as He promised—surely Adonijah shall be put to death today!”

verses.esv: So King Solomon sent Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and he struck him down, and he died.
verses.kjv: And king Solomon sent by the hand of Benaiah the son of Jehoiada; and he fell upon him that he died.
verses.bsb: So King Solomon sent the order to Benaiah son of Jehoiada, who struck down Adonijah, and he died.

verses.esv: And to Abiathar the priest the king said, “Go to Anathoth, to your estate, for you deserve death. But I will not at this time put you to death, because you carried the ark of the Lord GOD before David my father, and because you shared in all my father's affliction.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And unto Abiathar the priest said the king, Get thee to Anathoth, unto thine own fields; for thou art worthy of death: but I will not at this time put thee to death, because thou barest the ark of the Lord God before David my father, and because thou hast been afflicted in all wherein my father was afflicted.
verses.bsb: Then the king said to Abiathar the priest, “Go back to your fields in Anathoth. Even though you deserve to die, I will not put you to death at this time, since you carried the ark of the Lord GOD before my father David, and you suffered through all that my father suffered.”

verses.esv: So Solomon expelled Abiathar from being priest to the LORD, thus fulfilling the word of the LORD that he had spoken concerning the house of Eli in Shiloh.
verses.kjv: So Solomon thrust out Abiathar from being priest unto the Lord; that he might fulfil the word of the Lord, which he spake concerning the house of Eli in Shiloh.
verses.bsb: So Solomon banished Abiathar from the priesthood of the LORD and thus fulfilled the word that the LORD had spoken at Shiloh against the house of Eli.

verses.esv: When the news came to Joab—for Joab had supported Adonijah although he had not supported Absalom—Joab fled to the tent of the LORD and caught hold of the horns of the altar.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then tidings came to Joab: for Joab had turned after Adonijah, though he turned not after Absalom. And Joab fled unto the tabernacle of the Lord, and caught hold on the horns of the altar.
verses.bsb: When the news reached Joab, who had conspired with Adonijah but not with Absalom, he fled to the tent of the LORD and took hold of the horns of the altar.

verses.esv: And when it was told King Solomon, “Joab has fled to the tent of the LORD, and behold, he is beside the altar,” Solomon sent Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, saying, “Go, strike him down.”
verses.kjv: And it was told king Solomon that Joab was fled unto the tabernacle of the Lord; and, behold, he is by the altar. Then Solomon sent Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, saying, Go, fall upon him.
verses.bsb: It was reported to King Solomon: “Joab has fled to the tent of the LORD and is now beside the altar.”  So Solomon sent Benaiah son of Jehoiada, saying, “Go, strike him down!”

verses.esv: So Benaiah came to the tent of the LORD and said to him, “The king commands, ‘Come out.’” But he said, “No, I will die here.” Then Benaiah brought the king word again, saying, “Thus said Joab, and thus he answered me.”
verses.kjv: And Benaiah came to the tabernacle of the Lord, and said unto him, Thus saith the king, Come forth. And he said, Nay; but I will die here. And Benaiah brought the king word again, saying, Thus said Joab, and thus he answered me.
verses.bsb: And Benaiah entered the tent of the LORD and said to Joab, “The king says, ‘Come out!’”  But Joab replied, “No, I will die here.”  So Benaiah relayed the message to the king, saying, “This is how Joab answered me.”

verses.esv: The king replied to him, “Do as he has said, strike him down and bury him, and thus take away from me and from my father's house the guilt for the blood that Joab shed without cause.
verses.kjv: And the king said unto him, Do as he hath said, and fall upon him, and bury him; that thou mayest take away the innocent blood, which Joab shed, from me, and from the house of my father.
verses.bsb: And the king replied, “Do just as he says. Strike him down and bury him, and so remove from me and from the house of my father the innocent blood that Joab shed.

verses.esv: The LORD will bring back his bloody deeds on his own head, because, without the knowledge of my father David, he attacked and killed with the sword two men more righteous and better than himself, Abner the son of Ner, commander of the army of Israel, and Amasa the son of Jether, commander of the army of Judah.
verses.kjv: And the Lord shall return his blood upon his own head, who fell upon two men more righteous and better than he, and slew them with the sword, my father David not knowing thereof, to wit, Abner the son of Ner, captain of the host of Israel, and Amasa the son of Jether, captain of the host of Judah.
verses.bsb: The LORD will bring his bloodshed back upon his own head, for without the knowledge of my father David he struck down two men more righteous and better than he when he put to the sword Abner son of Ner, commander of Israel’s army, and Amasa son of Jether, commander of Judah’s army.

verses.esv: So shall their blood come back on the head of Joab and on the head of his descendants forever. But for David and for his descendants and for his house and for his throne there shall be peace from the LORD forevermore.”
verses.kjv: Their blood shall therefore return upon the head of Joab, and upon the head of his seed for ever: but upon David, and upon his seed, and upon his house, and upon his throne, shall there be peace for ever from the Lord.
verses.bsb: Their blood will come back upon the heads of Joab and his descendants forever; but for David, his descendants, his house, and his throne, there shall be peace from the LORD forever.”

verses.esv: Then Benaiah the son of Jehoiada went up and struck him down and put him to death. And he was buried in his own house in the wilderness.
verses.kjv: So Benaiah the son of Jehoiada went up, and fell upon him, and slew him: and he was buried in his own house in the wilderness.
verses.bsb: So Benaiah son of Jehoiada went up, struck down Joab, and killed him. He was buried at his own home in the wilderness.

verses.esv: The king put Benaiah the son of Jehoiada over the army in place of Joab, and the king put Zadok the priest in the place of Abiathar.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the king put Benaiah the son of Jehoiada in his room over the host: and Zadok the priest did the king put in the room of Abiathar.
verses.bsb: And the king appointed Benaiah son of Jehoiada in Joab’s place over the army, and he appointed Zadok the priest in Abiathar’s place.

verses.esv: Then the king sent and summoned Shimei and said to him, “Build yourself a house in Jerusalem and dwell there, and do not go out from there to any place whatever.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the king sent and called for Shimei, and said unto him, Build thee an house in Jerusalem, and dwell there, and go not forth thence any whither.
verses.bsb: Then the king summoned Shimei and said to him, “Build a house for yourself in Jerusalem and live there, but do not go anywhere else.

verses.esv: For on the day you go out and cross the brook Kidron, know for certain that you shall die. Your blood shall be on your own head.”
verses.kjv: For it shall be, that on the day thou goest out, and passest over the brook Kidron, thou shalt know for certain that thou shalt surely die: thy blood shall be upon thine own head.
verses.bsb: On the day you go out and cross the Kidron Valley, know for sure that you will die; your blood will be on your own head.”

verses.esv: And Shimei said to the king, “What you say is good; as my lord the king has said, so will your servant do.” So Shimei lived in Jerusalem many days.
verses.kjv: And Shimei said unto the king, The saying is good: as my lord the king hath said, so will thy servant do. And Shimei dwelt in Jerusalem many days.
verses.bsb: “The sentence is fair,” Shimei replied. “Your servant will do as my lord the king has spoken.” And Shimei lived in Jerusalem for a long time.

verses.esv: But it happened at the end of three years that two of Shimei's servants ran away to Achish, son of Maacah, king of Gath. And when it was told Shimei, “Behold, your servants are in Gath,”
verses.kjv: And it came to pass at the end of three years, that two of the servants of Shimei ran away unto Achish son of Maachah king of Gath. And they told Shimei, saying, Behold, thy servants be in Gath.
verses.bsb: After three years, however, two of Shimei’s slaves ran away to Achish son of Maacah, king of Gath. And Shimei was told, “Look, your slaves are in Gath.”

verses.esv: Shimei arose and saddled a donkey and went to Gath to Achish to seek his servants. Shimei went and brought his servants from Gath.
verses.kjv: And Shimei arose, and saddled his ass, and went to Gath to Achish to seek his servants: and Shimei went, and brought his servants from Gath.
verses.bsb: So Shimei saddled his donkey and set out to Achish at Gath in search of his slaves, and he brought them back from Gath.

verses.esv: And when Solomon was told that Shimei had gone from Jerusalem to Gath and returned,
verses.kjv: And it was told Solomon that Shimei had gone from Jerusalem to Gath, and was come again.
verses.bsb: When Solomon was told that Shimei had gone from Jerusalem to Gath and had returned,

verses.esv: the king sent and summoned Shimei and said to him, “Did I not make you swear by the LORD and solemnly warn you, saying, ‘Know for certain that on the day you go out and go to any place whatever, you shall die’? And you said to me, ‘What you say is good; I will obey.’
verses.kjv: And the king sent and called for Shimei, and said unto him, Did I not make thee to swear by the Lord, and protested unto thee, saying, Know for a certain, on the day thou goest out, and walkest abroad any whither, that thou shalt surely die? and thou saidst unto me, The word that I have heard is good.
verses.bsb: the king summoned Shimei and said to him, “Did I not make you swear by the LORD and warn you, ‘On the day you leave and go elsewhere, know for sure that you will die’? And you told me, ‘The sentence is fair; I will comply.’

verses.esv: Why then have you not kept your oath to the LORD and the commandment with which I commanded you?”
verses.kjv: Why then hast thou not kept the oath of the Lord, and the commandment that I have charged thee with?
verses.bsb: So why have you not kept your oath to the LORD and the command that I gave you?”

verses.esv: The king also said to Shimei, “You know in your own heart all the harm that you did to David my father. So the LORD will bring back your harm on your own head.
verses.kjv: The king said moreover to Shimei, Thou knowest all the wickedness which thine heart is privy to, that thou didst to David my father: therefore the Lord shall return thy wickedness upon thine own head;
verses.bsb: The king also said, “You know in your heart all the evil that you did to my father David. Therefore the LORD will bring your evil back upon your head.

verses.esv: But King Solomon shall be blessed, and the throne of David shall be established before the LORD forever.”
verses.kjv: And king Solomon shall be blessed, and the throne of David shall be established before the Lord for ever.
verses.bsb: But King Solomon will be blessed and David’s throne will remain secure before the LORD forever.”

verses.esv: Then the king commanded Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and he went out and struck him down, and he died.
verses.kjv: So the king commanded Benaiah the son of Jehoiada; which went out, and fell upon him, that he died. And the kingdom was established in the hand of Solomon.
verses.bsb: Then the king commanded Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and he went out and struck Shimei down, and he died. Thus the kingdom was firmly established in the hand of Solomon.

verses.esv: Solomon made a marriage alliance with Pharaoh king of Egypt. He took Pharaoh's daughter and brought her into the city of David until he had finished building his own house and the house of the LORD and the wall around Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: AND Solomon made affinity with Pharaoh king of Egypt, and took Pharaoh’s daughter, and brought her into the city of David, until he had made an end of building his own house, and the house of the Lord, and the wall of Jerusalem round about.
verses.bsb: Later, Solomon formed an alliance with Pharaoh king of Egypt by marrying his daughter. Solomon brought her to the City of David until he had finished building his palace and the house of the LORD, as well as the wall around Jerusalem.

verses.esv: The people were sacrificing at the high places, however, because no house had yet been built for the name of the LORD.
verses.kjv: Only the people sacrificed in high places, because there was no house built unto the name of the Lord, until those days.
verses.bsb: The people, however, were still sacrificing on the high places because a house for the Name of the LORD had not yet been built.

verses.esv: Solomon loved the LORD, walking in the statutes of David his father, only he sacrificed and made offerings at the high places.
verses.kjv: And Solomon loved the Lord, walking in the statutes of David his father: only he sacrificed and burnt incense in high places.
verses.bsb: And Solomon loved the LORD and walked in the statutes of his father David, except that he sacrificed and burned incense on the high places.

verses.esv: And the king went to Gibeon to sacrifice there, for that was the great high place. Solomon used to offer a thousand burnt offerings on that altar.
verses.kjv: And the king went to Gibeon to sacrifice there; for that was the great high place: a thousand burnt offerings did Solomon offer upon that altar.
verses.bsb: Now the king went to Gibeon to sacrifice there, for it was the great high place. Solomon offered a thousand burnt offerings on the altar there.

verses.esv: At Gibeon the LORD appeared to Solomon in a dream by night, and God said, “Ask what I shall give you.”
verses.kjv: ¶ In Gibeon the Lord appeared to Solomon in a dream by night: and God said, Ask what I shall give thee.
verses.bsb: One night at Gibeon the LORD appeared to Solomon in a dream, and God said, “Ask, and I will give it to you!”

verses.esv: And Solomon said, “You have shown great and steadfast love to your servant David my father, because he walked before you in faithfulness, in righteousness, and in uprightness of heart toward you. And you have kept for him this great and steadfast love and have given him a son to sit on his throne this day.
verses.kjv: And Solomon said, Thou hast shewed unto thy servant David my father great mercy, according as he walked before thee in truth, and in righteousness, and in uprightness of heart with thee; and thou hast kept for him this great kindness, that thou hast given him a son to sit on his throne, as it is this day.
verses.bsb: Solomon replied, “You have shown much loving devotion to Your servant, my father David, because he walked before You in faithfulness, righteousness, and uprightness of heart. And You have maintained this loving devotion by giving him a son to sit on his throne this very day.

verses.esv: And now, O LORD my God, you have made your servant king in place of David my father, although I am but a little child. I do not know how to go out or come in.
verses.kjv: And now, O Lord my God, thou hast made thy servant king instead of David my father: and I am but a little child: I know not how to go out or come in.
verses.bsb: And now, O LORD my God, You have made Your servant king in my father David’s place. But I am only a little child, not knowing how to go out or come in.

verses.esv: And your servant is in the midst of your people whom you have chosen, a great people, too many to be numbered or counted for multitude.
verses.kjv: And thy servant is in the midst of thy people which thou hast chosen, a great people, that cannot be numbered nor counted for multitude.
verses.bsb: Your servant is here among the people You have chosen, a people too numerous to count or number.

verses.esv: Give your servant therefore an understanding mind to govern your people, that I may discern between good and evil, for who is able to govern this your great people?”
verses.kjv: Give therefore thy servant an understanding heart to judge thy people, that I may discern between good and bad: for who is able to judge this thy so great a people?
verses.bsb: Therefore give Your servant an understanding heart to judge Your people and to discern between good and evil. For who is able to govern this great people of Yours?”

verses.esv: It pleased the Lord that Solomon had asked this.
verses.kjv: And the speech pleased the Lord, that Solomon had asked this thing.
verses.bsb: Now it pleased the Lord that Solomon had made this request.

verses.esv: And God said to him, “Because you have asked this, and have not asked for yourself long life or riches or the life of your enemies, but have asked for yourself understanding to discern what is right,
verses.kjv: And God said unto him, Because thou hast asked this thing, and hast not asked for thyself long life; neither hast asked riches for thyself, nor hast asked the life of thine enemies; but hast asked for thyself understanding to discern judgment;
verses.bsb: So God said to him, “Since you have asked for this instead of requesting long life or wealth for yourself or death for your enemies—but you have asked for discernment to administer justice—

verses.esv: behold, I now do according to your word. Behold, I give you a wise and discerning mind, so that none like you has been before you and none like you shall arise after you.
verses.kjv: Behold, I have done according to thy words: lo, I have given thee a wise and an understanding heart; so that there was none like thee before thee, neither after thee shall any arise like unto thee.
verses.bsb: behold, I will do what you have asked. I will give you a wise and discerning heart, so that there has never been nor will ever be another like you.

verses.esv: I give you also what you have not asked, both riches and honor, so that no other king shall compare with you, all your days.
verses.kjv: And I have also given thee that which thou hast not asked, both riches, and honour: so that there shall not be any among the kings like unto thee all thy days.
verses.bsb: Moreover, I will give you what you did not request—both riches and honor—so that during all your days no man in any kingdom will be your equal.

verses.esv: And if you will walk in my ways, keeping my statutes and my commandments, as your father David walked, then I will lengthen your days.”
verses.kjv: And if thou wilt walk in my ways, to keep my statutes and my commandments, as thy father David did walk, then I will lengthen thy days.
verses.bsb: So if you walk in My ways and keep My statutes and commandments, just as your father David did, I will prolong your days.”

verses.esv: And Solomon awoke, and behold, it was a dream. Then he came to Jerusalem and stood before the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and offered up burnt offerings and peace offerings, and made a feast for all his servants.
verses.kjv: And Solomon awoke; and, behold, it was a dream. And he came to Jerusalem, and stood before the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and offered up burnt offerings, and offered peace offerings, and made a feast to all his servants.
verses.bsb: Then Solomon awoke, and indeed it had been a dream. So he returned to Jerusalem, stood before the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings. Then he held a feast for all his servants.

verses.esv: Then two prostitutes came to the king and stood before him.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then came there two women, that were harlots, unto the king, and stood before him.
verses.bsb: At that time two prostitutes came to the king and stood before him.

verses.esv: The one woman said, “Oh, my lord, this woman and I live in the same house, and I gave birth to a child while she was in the house.
verses.kjv: And the one woman said, O my lord, I and this woman dwell in one house; and I was delivered of a child with her in the house.
verses.bsb: One woman said, “Please, my lord, this woman and I live in the same house, and I gave birth while she was in the house.

verses.esv: Then on the third day after I gave birth, this woman also gave birth. And we were alone. There was no one else with us in the house; only we two were in the house.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass the third day after that I was delivered, that this woman was delivered also: and we were together; there was no stranger with us in the house, save we two in the house.
verses.bsb: On the third day after I gave birth, this woman also had a baby. We were alone, with no one in the house but the two of us.

verses.esv: And this woman's son died in the night, because she lay on him.
verses.kjv: And this woman’s child died in the night; because she overlaid it.
verses.bsb: During the night this woman’s son died because she rolled over on him.

verses.esv: And she arose at midnight and took my son from beside me, while your servant slept, and laid him at her breast, and laid her dead son at my breast.
verses.kjv: And she arose at midnight, and took my son from beside me, while thine handmaid slept, and laid it in her bosom, and laid her dead child in my bosom.
verses.bsb: So she got up in the middle of the night and took my son from my side while I was asleep. She laid him in her bosom and put her dead son at my bosom.

verses.esv: When I rose in the morning to nurse my child, behold, he was dead. But when I looked at him closely in the morning, behold, he was not the child that I had borne.”
verses.kjv: And when I rose in the morning to give my child suck, behold, it was dead: but when I had considered it in the morning, behold, it was not my son, which I did bear.
verses.bsb: The next morning, when I got up to nurse my son, I discovered he was dead. But when I examined him, I realized that he was not the son I had borne.”

verses.esv: But the other woman said, “No, the living child is mine, and the dead child is yours.” The first said, “No, the dead child is yours, and the living child is mine.” Thus they spoke before the king.
verses.kjv: And the other woman said, Nay; but the living is my son, and the dead is thy son. And this said, No; but the dead is thy son, and the living is my son. Thus they spake before the king.
verses.bsb: “No,” said the other woman, “the living one is my son and the dead one is your son.”  But the first woman insisted, “No, the dead one is yours and the living one is mine.” So they argued before the king.

verses.esv: Then the king said, “The one says, ‘This is my son that is alive, and your son is dead’; and the other says, ‘No; but your son is dead, and my son is the living one.’”
verses.kjv: Then said the king, The one saith, This is my son that liveth, and thy son is the dead: and the other saith, Nay; but thy son is the dead, and my son is the living.
verses.bsb: Then the king replied, “This woman says, ‘My son is alive and yours is dead,’ but that woman says, ‘No, your son is dead and mine is alive.’”

verses.esv: And the king said, “Bring me a sword.” So a sword was brought before the king.
verses.kjv: And the king said, Bring me a sword. And they brought a sword before the king.
verses.bsb: The king continued, “Bring me a sword.” So they brought him a sword,

verses.esv: And the king said, “Divide the living child in two, and give half to the one and half to the other.”
verses.kjv: And the king said, Divide the living child in two, and give half to the one, and half to the other.
verses.bsb: and the king declared, “Cut the living child in two and give half to one and half to the other.”

verses.esv: Then the woman whose son was alive said to the king, because her heart yearned for her son, “Oh, my lord, give her the living child, and by no means put him to death.” But the other said, “He shall be neither mine nor yours; divide him.”
verses.kjv: Then spake the woman whose the living child was unto the king, for her bowels yearned upon her son, and she said, O my lord, give her the living child, and in no wise slay it. But the other said, Let it be neither mine nor thine, but divide it.
verses.bsb: Then the woman whose son was alive spoke to the king because she yearned with compassion for her son. “Please, my lord,” she said, “give her the living baby. Do not kill him!”  But the other woman said, “He will be neither mine nor yours. Cut him in two!”

verses.esv: Then the king answered and said, “Give the living child to the first woman, and by no means put him to death; she is his mother.”
verses.kjv: Then the king answered and said, Give her the living child, and in no wise slay it: she is the mother thereof.
verses.bsb: Then the king gave his ruling: “Give the living baby to the first woman. By no means should you kill him; she is his mother.”

verses.esv: And all Israel heard of the judgment that the king had rendered, and they stood in awe of the king, because they perceived that the wisdom of God was in him to do justice.
verses.kjv: And all Israel heard of the judgment which the king had judged; and they feared the king: for they saw that the wisdom of God was in him, to do judgment.
verses.bsb: When all Israel heard of the judgment the king had given, they stood in awe of him, for they saw that the wisdom of God was in him to administer justice.

verses.esv: King Solomon was king over all Israel,
verses.kjv: SO king Solomon was king over all Israel.
verses.bsb: So King Solomon ruled over Israel,

verses.esv: and these were his high officials: Azariah the son of Zadok was the priest;
verses.kjv: And these were the princes which he had; Azariah the son of Zadok the priest,
verses.bsb: and these were his chief officials:  Azariah son of Zadok was the priest;

verses.esv: Elihoreph and Ahijah the sons of Shisha were secretaries; Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud was recorder;
verses.kjv: Elihoreph and Ahiah, the sons of Shisha, scribes; Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud, the recorder.
verses.bsb: Elihoreph and Ahijah, the sons of Shisha, were secretaries;   Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was the recorder;

verses.esv: Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was in command of the army; Zadok and Abiathar were priests;
verses.kjv: And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the host: and Zadok and Abiathar were the priests:
verses.bsb: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was in charge of the army;   Zadok and Abiathar were priests;

verses.esv: Azariah the son of Nathan was over the officers; Zabud the son of Nathan was priest and king's friend;
verses.kjv: And Azariah the son of Nathan was over the officers: and Zabud the son of Nathan was principal officer, and the king’s friend:
verses.bsb: Azariah son of Nathan was in charge of the governors;   Zabud son of Nathan was a priest and adviser to the king;

verses.esv: Ahishar was in charge of the palace; and Adoniram the son of Abda was in charge of the forced labor.
verses.kjv: And Ahishar was over the household: and Adoniram the son of Abda was over the tribute.
verses.bsb: Ahishar was in charge of the palace;   and Adoniram son of Abda was in charge of the forced labor.

verses.esv: Solomon had twelve officers over all Israel, who provided food for the king and his household. Each man had to make provision for one month in the year.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Solomon had twelve officers over all Israel, which provided victuals for the king and his household: each man his month in a year made provision.
verses.bsb: Solomon had twelve governors over all Israel to provide food for the king and his household. Each one would arrange provisions for one month of the year,

verses.esv: These were their names: Ben-hur, in the hill country of Ephraim;
verses.kjv: And these are their names: The son of Hur, in mount Ephraim:
verses.bsb: and these were their names:  Ben-hur in the hill country of Ephraim;

verses.esv: Ben-deker, in Makaz, Shaalbim, Beth-shemesh, and Elonbeth-hanan;
verses.kjv: The son of Dekar, in Makaz, and in Shaalbim, and Beth-shemesh, and Elon-beth-hanan:
verses.bsb: Ben-deker in Makaz, in Shaalbim, in Beth-shemesh, and in Elon-beth-hanan;

verses.esv: Ben-hesed, in Arubboth (to him belonged Socoh and all the land of Hepher);
verses.kjv: The son of Hesed, in Aruboth; to him pertained Sochoh, and all the land of Hepher:
verses.bsb: Ben-hesed in Arubboth (Socoh and all the land of Hepher belonged to him);

verses.esv: Ben-abinadab, in all Naphath-dor (he had Taphath the daughter of Solomon as his wife);
verses.kjv: The son of Abinadab, in all the region of Dor; which had Taphath the daughter of Solomon to wife:
verses.bsb: Ben-abinadab in Naphath-dor (Taphath, a daughter of Solomon, was his wife);

verses.esv: Baana the son of Ahilud, in Taanach, Megiddo, and all Beth-shean that is beside Zarethan below Jezreel, and from Beth-shean to Abel-meholah, as far as the other side of Jokmeam;
verses.kjv: Baana the son of Ahilud; to him pertained Taanach and Megiddo, and all Beth-shean, which is by Zartanah beneath Jezreel, from Beth-shean to Abel-meholah, even unto the place that is beyond Jokneam:
verses.bsb: Baana son of Ahilud in Taanach, in Megiddo, and in all of Beth-shean next to Zarethan below Jezreel, from Beth-shean to Abel-meholah and on past Jokmeam;

verses.esv: Ben-geber, in Ramoth-gilead (he had the villages of Jair the son of Manasseh, which are in Gilead, and he had the region of Argob, which is in Bashan, sixty great cities with walls and bronze bars);
verses.kjv: The son of Geber, in Ramoth-gilead; to him pertained the towns of Jair the son of Manasseh, which are in Gilead; to him also pertained the region of Argob, which is in Bashan, threescore great cities with walls and brasen bars:
verses.bsb: Ben-geber in Ramoth-gilead (the villages of Jair son of Manasseh in Gilead belonged to him, as well as the region of Argob in Bashan with its sixty great cities with walls and bronze bars);

verses.esv: Ahinadab the son of Iddo, in Mahanaim;
verses.kjv: Ahinadab the son of Iddo had Mahanaim:
verses.bsb: Ahinadab son of Iddo in Mahanaim;

verses.esv: Ahimaaz, in Naphtali (he had taken Basemath the daughter of Solomon as his wife);
verses.kjv: Ahimaaz was in Naphtali; he also took Basmath the daughter of Solomon to wife:
verses.bsb: Ahimaaz in Naphtali (he had married Basemath, a daughter of Solomon);

verses.esv: Baana the son of Hushai, in Asher and Bealoth;
verses.kjv: Baanah the son of Hushai was in Asher and in Aloth:
verses.bsb: Baana son of Hushai in Asher and in Aloth;

verses.esv: Jehoshaphat the son of Paruah, in Issachar;
verses.kjv: Jehoshaphat the son of Paruah, in Issachar:
verses.bsb: Jehoshaphat son of Paruah in Issachar;

verses.esv: Shimei the son of Ela, in Benjamin;
verses.kjv: Shimei the son of Elah, in Benjamin:
verses.bsb: Shimei son of Ela in Benjamin;

verses.esv: Geber the son of Uri, in the land of Gilead, the country of Sihon king of the Amorites and of Og king of Bashan. And there was one governor who was over the land.
verses.kjv: Geber the son of Uri was in the country of Gilead, in the country of Sihon king of the Amorites, and of Og king of Bashan; and he was the only officer which was in the land.
verses.bsb: Geber son of Uri in the land of Gilead, including the territories of Sihon king of the Amorites and of Og king of Bashan.   There was also one governor in the land of Judah.

verses.esv: Judah and Israel were as many as the sand by the sea. They ate and drank and were happy.
verses.kjv: ¶ Judah and Israel were many, as the sand which is by the sea in multitude, eating and drinking, and making merry.
verses.bsb: The people of Judah and Israel were as numerous as the sand on the seashore, and they were eating and drinking and rejoicing.

verses.esv: Solomon ruled over all the kingdoms from the Euphrates to the land of the Philistines and to the border of Egypt. They brought tribute and served Solomon all the days of his life.
verses.kjv: And Solomon reigned over all kingdoms from the river unto the land of the Philistines, and unto the border of Egypt: they brought presents, and served Solomon all the days of his life.
verses.bsb: And Solomon reigned over all the kingdoms from the Euphrates to the land of the Philistines, as far as the border of Egypt. These kingdoms offered tribute and served Solomon all the days of his life.

verses.esv: Solomon's provision for one day was thirty cors of fine flour and sixty cors of meal,
verses.kjv: ¶ And Solomon’s provision for one day was thirty measures of fine flour, and threescore measures of meal,
verses.bsb: Solomon’s provisions for a single day were thirty cors of fine flour, sixty cors of meal,

verses.esv: ten fat oxen, and twenty pasture-fed cattle, a hundred sheep, besides deer, gazelles, roebucks, and fattened fowl.
verses.kjv: Ten fat oxen, and twenty oxen out of the pastures, and an hundred sheep, beside harts, and roebucks, and fallowdeer, and fatted fowl.
verses.bsb: ten fat oxen, twenty range oxen, and a hundred sheep, as well as deer, gazelles, roebucks, and fattened poultry.

verses.esv: For he had dominion over all the region west of the Euphrates from Tiphsah to Gaza, over all the kings west of the Euphrates. And he had peace on all sides around him.
verses.kjv: For he had dominion over all the region on this side the river, from Tiphsah even to Azzah, over all the kings on this side the river: and he had peace on all sides round about him.
verses.bsb: For Solomon had dominion over everything west of the Euphrates—over all the kingdoms from Tiphsah to Gaza—and he had peace on all sides.

verses.esv: And Judah and Israel lived in safety, from Dan even to Beersheba, every man under his vine and under his fig tree, all the days of Solomon.
verses.kjv: And Judah and Israel dwelt safely, every man under his vine and under his fig tree, from Dan even to Beer-sheba, all the days of Solomon.
verses.bsb: Throughout the days of Solomon, Judah and Israel dwelt securely from Dan to Beersheba, each man under his own vine and his own fig tree.

verses.esv: Solomon also had 40,000 stalls of horses for his chariots, and 12,000 horsemen.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Solomon had forty thousand stalls of horses for his chariots, and twelve thousand horsemen.
verses.bsb: Solomon had 4,000 stalls for his chariot horses and 12,000 horses.

verses.esv: And those officers supplied provisions for King Solomon, and for all who came to King Solomon's table, each one in his month. They let nothing be lacking.
verses.kjv: And those officers provided victual for king Solomon, and for all that came unto king Solomon’s table, every man in his month: they lacked nothing.
verses.bsb: Each month the governors in turn provided food for King Solomon and all who came to his table. They saw to it that nothing was lacking.

verses.esv: Barley also and straw for the horses and swift steeds they brought to the place where it was required, each according to his duty.
verses.kjv: Barley also and straw for the horses and dromedaries brought they unto the place where the officers were, every man according to his charge.
verses.bsb: Each one also brought to the required place their quotas of barley and straw for the chariot horses and other horses.

verses.esv: And God gave Solomon wisdom and understanding beyond measure, and breadth of mind like the sand on the seashore,
verses.kjv: ¶ And God gave Solomon wisdom and understanding exceeding much, and largeness of heart, even as the sand that is on the sea shore.
verses.bsb: And God gave Solomon wisdom, exceedingly deep insight, and understanding beyond measure, like the sand on the seashore.

verses.esv: so that Solomon's wisdom surpassed the wisdom of all the people of the east and all the wisdom of Egypt.
verses.kjv: And Solomon’s wisdom excelled the wisdom of all the children of the east country, and all the wisdom of Egypt.
verses.bsb: Solomon’s wisdom was greater than that of all the men of the East, greater than all the wisdom of Egypt.

verses.esv: For he was wiser than all other men, wiser than Ethan the Ezrahite, and Heman, Calcol, and Darda, the sons of Mahol, and his fame was in all the surrounding nations.
verses.kjv: For he was wiser than all men; than Ethan the Ezrahite, and Heman, and Chalcol, and Darda, the sons of Mahol: and his fame was in all nations round about.
verses.bsb: He was wiser than all men—wiser than Ethan the Ezrahite, and wiser than Heman, Calcol, and Darda, the sons of Mahol. And his fame spread throughout the surrounding nations.

verses.esv: He also spoke 3,000 proverbs, and his songs were 1,005.
verses.kjv: And he spake three thousand proverbs: and his songs were a thousand and five.
verses.bsb: Solomon composed three thousand proverbs, and his songs numbered a thousand and five.

verses.esv: He spoke of trees, from the cedar that is in Lebanon to the hyssop that grows out of the wall. He spoke also of beasts, and of birds, and of reptiles, and of fish.
verses.kjv: And he spake of trees, from the cedar tree that is in Lebanon even unto the hyssop that springeth out of the wall: he spake also of beasts, and of fowl, and of creeping things, and of fishes.
verses.bsb: He spoke of trees, from the cedar in Lebanon to the hyssop growing in the wall, and he taught about animals, birds, reptiles, and fish.

verses.esv: And people of all nations came to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and from all the kings of the earth, who had heard of his wisdom.
verses.kjv: And there came of all people to hear the wisdom of Solomon, from all kings of the earth, which had heard of his wisdom.
verses.bsb: So men of all nations came to listen to Solomon’s wisdom, sent by all the kings of the earth, who had heard of his wisdom.

verses.esv: Now Hiram king of Tyre sent his servants to Solomon when he heard that they had anointed him king in place of his father, for Hiram always loved David.
verses.kjv: AND Hiram king of Tyre sent his servants unto Solomon; for he had heard that they had anointed him king in the room of his father: for Hiram was ever a lover of David.
verses.bsb: Now when Hiram king of Tyre heard that Solomon had been anointed king in his father’s place, he sent envoys to Solomon; for Hiram had always been a friend of David.

verses.esv: And Solomon sent word to Hiram,
verses.kjv: And Solomon sent to Hiram, saying,
verses.bsb: And Solomon relayed this message to Hiram:

verses.esv: “You know that David my father could not build a house for the name of the LORD his God because of the warfare with which his enemies surrounded him, until the LORD put them under the soles of his feet.
verses.kjv: Thou knowest how that David my father could not build an house unto the name of the Lord his God for the wars which were about him on every side, until the Lord put them under the soles of his feet.
verses.bsb: “As you are well aware, due to the wars waged on all sides against my father David, he could not build a house for the Name of the LORD his God until the LORD had put his enemies under his feet.

verses.esv: But now the LORD my God has given me rest on every side. There is neither adversary nor misfortune.
verses.kjv: But now the Lord my God hath given me rest on every side, so that there is neither adversary nor evil occurrent.
verses.bsb: But now the LORD my God has given me rest on every side, and there is no adversary or crisis.

verses.esv: And so I intend to build a house for the name of the LORD my God, as the LORD said to David my father, ‘Your son, whom I will set on your throne in your place, shall build the house for my name.’
verses.kjv: And, behold, I purpose to build an house unto the name of the Lord my God, as the Lord spake unto David my father, saying, Thy son, whom I will set upon thy throne in thy room, he shall build an house unto my name.
verses.bsb: So behold, I plan to build a house for the Name of the LORD my God, according to what the LORD said to my father David: ‘I will put your son on your throne in your place, and he will build the house for My Name.’

verses.esv: Now therefore command that cedars of Lebanon be cut for me. And my servants will join your servants, and I will pay you for your servants such wages as you set, for you know that there is no one among us who knows how to cut timber like the Sidonians.”
verses.kjv: Now therefore command thou that they hew me cedar trees out of Lebanon; and my servants shall be with thy servants: and unto thee will I give hire for thy servants according to all that thou shalt appoint: for thou knowest that there is not among us any that can skill to hew timber like unto the Sidonians.
verses.bsb: Now therefore, order that cedars of Lebanon be cut down for me. My servants will be with your servants, and I will pay your servants whatever wages you set, for you know that there are none among us as skilled in logging as the Sidonians.”

verses.esv: As soon as Hiram heard the words of Solomon, he rejoiced greatly and said, “Blessed be the LORD this day, who has given to David a wise son to be over this great people.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, when Hiram heard the words of Solomon, that he rejoiced greatly, and said, Blessed be the Lord this day, which hath given unto David a wise son over this great people.
verses.bsb: When Hiram received Solomon’s message, he rejoiced greatly and said, “Blessed be the LORD this day! He has given David a wise son over this great people!”

verses.esv: And Hiram sent to Solomon, saying, “I have heard the message that you have sent to me. I am ready to do all you desire in the matter of cedar and cypress timber.
verses.kjv: And Hiram sent to Solomon, saying, I have considered the things which thou sentest to me for: and I will do all thy desire concerning timber of cedar, and concerning timber of fir.
verses.bsb: Then Hiram sent a reply to Solomon, saying:  “I have received your message; I will do all you desire regarding the cedar and cypress timber.

verses.esv: My servants shall bring it down to the sea from Lebanon, and I will make it into rafts to go by sea to the place you direct. And I will have them broken up there, and you shall receive it. And you shall meet my wishes by providing food for my household.”
verses.kjv: My servants shall bring them down from Lebanon unto the sea: and I will convey them by sea in floats unto the place that thou shalt appoint me, and will cause them to be discharged there, and thou shalt receive them: and thou shalt accomplish my desire, in giving food for my household.
verses.bsb: My servants will haul the logs from Lebanon to the Sea, and I will float them as rafts by sea to the place you specify. There I will separate the logs, and you can take them away. And in exchange, you can meet my needs by providing my household with food.”

verses.esv: So Hiram supplied Solomon with all the timber of cedar and cypress that he desired,
verses.kjv: So Hiram gave Solomon cedar trees and fir trees according to all his desire.
verses.bsb: So Hiram provided Solomon with all the cedar and cypress timber he wanted,

verses.esv: while Solomon gave Hiram 20,000 cors of wheat as food for his household, and 20,000 cors of beaten oil. Solomon gave this to Hiram year by year.
